html:
Exclusive to:

DayNew MoonWaxing MoonFull MoonWaning MoonCaveGlassed

The Moon Dial is a craftable structure exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It is found in the Magic Filter or Structures Filter, requires a Blue Moonlens, 2 Moon Rock and 2 Ice to craft, and a Prestihatitator to prototype.

The Moon Dial can be used to track the Moon Cycle, which is reflected in the water inside the structure. The closer the world is to a full moon, the higher the water level will be, with the Moon Dial overflowing during a full moon. The Moon Dial also provides light depending on the current moon phase, with the new moon causing the Moon Dial to emit no light, and the full moon causing it to emit the most light.

The Moon Dial shows the Moon Cycle for any time of the day, but during nighttime, the player may inspect the dial for a different quote, depending on where the moon is in the cycle. A Moon Dial built in the Caves will not work, and character examination quotes will be different.

The Moon Dial can be useful when playing as Woodie, as it can be a more obvious indicator of when a Full Moon is about to occur.

When the Mysterious Energy is summoned, the Moon Dial will glass over. After the Celestial Champion is defeated, the Moon Dial will drop a Moon Shard.

Skill Tree (Wendy)

Lunar Sisterhood III

The Lunar Sisterhood III Skill allows Wendy to turn Abigail into her Gestalt form at the nights of waxing or full moon, by mutating her at the Moon Dial. Wendy can turn Abigail back into her ghostly form, by mutating her back at waning and new moon.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

Woven - Elegant
Art Nouveau Collection
Nouveau Moon Dial
New moon, new look!

Trivia

  • The Moon Dial was introduced in the Arts and Crafts update.
  • Curiously, Wolfgang's quote says he cannot see his own reflection in the pool, while Wortox says he can see his.

Assets

Moon DialNouveau Moon Dial

  • Built
  • The Moon Dial on a waxing crescent.
  • The Moon Dial on a waxing half moon.
  • The Moon Dial on a waxing gibbous.
  • The Moon Dial on a full moon.
  • The Moon Dial on a moonstorm.

  • Built

Gallery

  • Moon Dial as seen in the poster for the Arts and Crafts update.
  • The Moon Dial as seen in the game. Note that the half moon is reflected on the Moon Dial.
  • The Moon Dial as seen on the map.
  • Dial test animation from Rhymes With Play #280.
  • Dial animations Concept Art from Rhymes With Play #280.

“Water amplifies the science, allowing us to measure the moon.”
–Wilson, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“What the heck? I can still see the moon!”
–Willow, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Wolfgang see moon reflection, but not his mighty self!”
–Wolfgang, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Does it reflect from the other side too, Abigail?”
–Wendy, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“THE MOON IS INORGANIC AND GOOD”
–WX-78, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Something very odd is going on here.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Do I really need to be reminded of the moon during the day?”
–Woodie, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“The gem conjures water springs in conjunction with the "moon's" cycles.”
–Maxwell, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Mani's visage remains, even in the light öf day.”
–Wigfrid, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Hey, Ms. Wicker! How come we can still see the moon?”
–Webber, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“I hope the birds get to enjoy it, too.”
–Warly, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Night Ball looker”
–Wormwood, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Must be broke. I can still see the moon.”
–Winona, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“What a handsome devil I see in the water!”
–Wortox, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Special moon pond.”
–Wurt, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Huh? I can see the moon!”
–Walter, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“Why worry about the moon? I've got enough to contend with down here.”
–Wanda, when examining the Moon Dial during the day
“It's a new moon.”
–Wilson
“A new moon! Great, I hated the old one!”
–Willow
“Moon is hiding.”
–Wolfgang
“A new moon looms above us.”
–Wendy
“THE MOON IS TAKING A BREAK FROM LOOKING AT ORGANICS”
–WX-78
“The moon's cycle begins anew.”
–Wickerbottom
“The moon's gone for now. Phew.”
–Woodie
“The... "moon"... has retreated for now.”
–Maxwell
“Mani's retired tö his realm för the eve.”
–Wigfrid
“New moon! Neat!”
–Webber
“The new moon's arrived.”
–Warly
“No Night Ball”
–Wormwood
“Brand spanking new moon.”
–Winona
“A newest moon!”
–Wortox
“Moon not swimming tonight?”
–Wurt
“I know all the moon phases, this one's a new moon.”
–Walter
“It's a new moon. I'd best keep my wits about me...”
–Wanda
“The moon is waxing.”
–Wilson
“The moon is waxing, unlike me.”
–Willow
“Tiny moon is get bigger!”
–Wolfgang
“The moon waxes before us.”
–Wendy
“THE MOON IS CHARGING UP”
–WX-78
“The moon is waxing.”
–Wickerbottom
“The moon's waxing again.”
–Woodie
“The "moon" is waxing.”
–Maxwell
“Mani's pöwer gröws!”
–Wigfrid
“The moon is growing!”
–Webber
“The moon is waxing.”
–Warly
“Night Ball getting bigger”
–Wormwood
“It's waxing.”
–Winona
“It's waxing, yes oh yes it is.”
–Wortox
“Grow big, moon!”
–Wurt
“It's a waxing moon.”
–Walter
“The moon is waxing.”
–Wanda
“It's a full moon.”
–Wilson
“That's as full as it gets.”
–Willow
“Moon is very mighty!”
–Wolfgang
“A full moon looms overhead. Watchful.”
–Wendy
“MOON AT MAXIMUM POWER”
–WX-78
“This cycle of the moon has drawn to a close.”
–Wickerbottom
“Not a good night to have a curse, but then when's it ever?”
–Woodie
“The "moon" is full in the sky.”
–Maxwell
“Mani claims the skies!”
–Wigfrid
“Wow! Full moon!”
–Webber
“The full moon's arrived.”
–Warly
“Big Night Ball!”
–Wormwood
“Full as can be.”
–Winona
“Hyuyu, I feel a bit loony!”
–Wortox
“Big bouncy moon, florp!”
–Wurt
“The full moon, a.k.a. best time to spot a werewolf!”
–Walter
“Oh good, a full moon!”
–Wanda
“The moon is waning.”
–Wilson
“The moon's outta here!”
–Willow
“Moon is get sleepy.”
–Wolfgang
“The moon is on the wane.”
–Wendy
“THE MOON IS ON COOLDOWN”
–WX-78
“The moon is waning.”
–Wickerbottom
“The moon's waning. Time for some peace and quiet.”
–Woodie
“The "moon" is on the wane.”
–Maxwell
“Mani's pöwer is ön the wane.”
–Wigfrid
“The moon is shrinking!”
–Webber
“The moon is waning.”
–Warly
“Night Ball getting smaller”
–Wormwood
“It's waning.”
–Winona
“It's on the wane, oh yes indeed.”
–Wortox
“Moon look smaller tonight.”
–Wurt
“It's a waning moon.”
–Walter
“The moon is waning.”
–Wanda
“There's no moon down here to measure.”
–Wilson, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“Doesn't work down here.”
–Willow, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“Moon is shy in cave!”
–Wolfgang, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“Caves. The moon's one weakness.”
–Wendy, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“THE MOON CAN'T SEE US HERE”
–WX-78, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“It appears to lose functionality in subterranean conditions.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“We're free from the moon's reach down here.”
–Woodie, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“The cave provides sanctuary from the "moon's" prying eyes.”
–Maxwell, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“Mani cannöt see us here.”
–Wigfrid, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“The moon can't fit in a cave! I think.”
–Webber, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“It doesn't seem very useful down here.”
–Warly, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“Oh. No Night Ball here”
–Wormwood, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“It was impractical to build this here.”
–Winona, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“It was so silly to build it down here!”
–Wortox, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“This not good spot.”
–Wurt, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“I can't see through stone... or can I? ...Nope.”
–Walter, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“I won't waste my time trying to see the moon down here.”
–Wanda, when examining the Moon Dial in the caves
“I have the strangest feeling I'm being watched.”
–Wilson
“I feel like it's staring at me.”
–Willow
“M-maybe is not good night for looking up at sky.”
–Wolfgang
“It's staring at me.”
–Wendy
“THEY CAN SEE US”
–WX-78
“How curious, the water reacts similarly to what I observed in the grotto.”
–Wickerbottom
“Wait a minute, I don't think that's ice...”
–Woodie
“I can feel its eye upon me... watching...”
–Maxwell
“By the göds! The water hath turned tö sölid glass!”
–Wigfrid
“It looks like ice, but we think it's actually glass!”
–Webber
“Mon dieu, the water has turned to glass!”
–Warly
“Hello!”
–Wormwood
“Why do I get the feeling something's watchin' me...”
–Winona
“They certainly are keeping a watchful eye over us, hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Glurph... something watching.”
–Wurt
“Wait, this isn't ice... it's glass!”
–Walter
“Oh botheration, THIS timeline again...”
–Wanda
The Moon Dial is a craftable structure exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It is found in the Magic Filter or Structures Filter, requires a Blue Moonlens, 2 Moon Rock and 2 Ice to craft, and a Prestihatitator to prototype.
The Moon Dial can be used to track the Moon Cycle, which is reflected in the water inside the structure. The closer the world is to a full moon, the higher the water level will be, with the Moon Dial overflowing during a full moon. The Moon Dial also provides light depending on the current moon phase, with the new moon causing the Moon Dial to emit no light, and the full moon causing it to emit the most light.
The Moon Dial shows the Moon Cycle for any time of the day, but during nighttime, the player may inspect the dial for a different quote, depending on where the moon is in the cycle. A Moon Dial built in the Caves will not work, and character examination quotes will be different.
The Moon Dial can be useful when playing as Woodie, as it can be a more obvious indicator of when a Full Moon is about to occur.
When the Mysterious Energy is summoned, the Moon Dial will glass over. After the Celestial Champion is defeated, the Moon Dial will drop a Moon Shard.
Skill Tree (Wendy)[edit | edit source]
Lunar Sisterhood III[edit | edit source]
The Lunar Sisterhood III Skill allows Wendy to turn Abigail into her Gestalt form at the nights of waxing or full moon, by mutating her at the Moon Dial. Wendy can turn Abigail back into her ghostly form, by mutating her back at waning and new moon.

It is found in the [[Magic Filter]] or [[Structures Filter]], requires a [[Moonlens|Blue Moonlens]], 2 [[Moon Rock]] and 2 [[Ice]] to craft, and a [[Prestihatitator]] to prototype. The Moon Dial can be used to track the [[Moon Cycle/DST|Moon Cycle]], which is reflected in the water inside the structure. The closer the world is to a full moon, the higher the water level will be, with the Moon Dial overflowing during a full moon. The Moon Dial also provides light depending on the current moon phase, with the new moon causing the Moon Dial to emit no light, and the full moon causing it to emit the most light. The Moon Dial shows the Moon Cycle for any time of the day, but during nighttime, the player may inspect the dial for a different quote, depending on where the moon is in the cycle. A Moon Dial built in the Caves will not work, and character examination quotes will be different. The Moon Dial can be useful when playing as [[Woodie]], as it can be a more obvious indicator of when a Full Moon is about to occur. When the [[Mysterious Energy]] is summoned, the Moon Dial will glass over. After the [[Celestial Champion]] is defeated, the Moon Dial will drop a [[Moon Shard]]. == {{Pic|32|Insight}} Skill Tree (Wendy) == === {{PicDark|32|Lunar Sisterhood III}} Lunar Sisterhood III=== The [[Lunar Sisterhood III]] Skill allows [[Wendy/Don't Starve Together|Wendy]] to turn [[Abigail/DST|Abigail]] into her Gestalt form at the nights of waxing or full moon, by mutating her at the Moon Dial.
